title: banksia occidentalis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Banksia occidentalis subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Western Banksia, is a shrub species within the Banksia genus. This plant is native to Western Australia and is characterized by its large, conical flower spikes and serrated leaves. The medicinal properties of Banksia occidentalis subsp. occidentalis include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's herbal preparations include infusions, teas, and extracts, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function.
